title: #49 — PBI ids can silently duplicate: add the uniqueness check, then repair the live P50 collision
action: TRIAGE ESTABLISHED THE REPORTED CAUSE IS WRONG — read CONTEXT.md D1 before coding. The issue says ids are allocated 'max + 1' and concurrent readers collide. There is NO allocator code at all: `.bee/bin/lib/backlog.mjs` only parses, counts, ranks and badges `docs/backlog.md`, and `bee backlog add` writes a DIFFERENT store (`.bee/backlog.jsonl`, the friction feed). The id rule lives in prose (`skills/bee-scribing/references/scribing-reference.md` §Product Backlog, 'next free integer, never reused') and is executed by an agent editing a markdown table. The committed duplicate proves it is not a race: `docs/backlog.md` carries TWO rows with id `P50` — one added 2026-07-17 by commit 3cc1e91, the other 2026-07-18 by 560b81e, a day apart on different branches, each author computing 'next free' from a snapshot predating the other. A lock would prevent nothing. WHAT IS MISSING IS A CHECK. STEP 1, RED-FIRST: add a uniqueness assertion over the parsed rows in `.bee/bin/lib/backlog.mjs` (the walkers `readBacklogCounts`/`rankBacklog` already visit every row — reuse that walk, do not add a second parse), exposed so the chain can run it, and CAPTURE IT FAILING and naming `P50` and both its line numbers on the CURRENT file, before you repair anything. STEP 2: wire it into `scripts/run_verify.mjs` EXTRA_SUITES and pin it in `scripts/test_verify_manifest.mjs` (MANDATORY_SUITES / MANDATORY_SUITE_ARGS), raising SUITE_FLOOR_COUNT. STEP 3: repair the live duplicate per D2 — renumber the LATER row (the 2026-07-18 one, 'A Codex session runs bee...') to the next genuinely free id, never reusing a vacated number; grep `docs/`, `README.md` and `.bee/` for citations of that row's old id and rewire any that exist. STEP 4: `skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_backlog_capture.mjs` currently PINS the duplicate as acceptable ('readBacklogCounts counts duplicate IDs honestly (row-by-row, dedup is grooming prose)'). That assertion is about COUNTING and may well stay true — read it, decide honestly whether it survives, and if it must change, rewrite it with its reason rather than deleting it. Counting duplicates honestly and REFUSING them in the chain are not in conflict; say so in the code. Do NOT invent a `bee backlog next-id` allocator — explicitly out of scope (D1).

truths: red evidence: the new check FAILING on the pre-repair file, naming P50 and both row line numbers — quoted
green evidence: the check passing after the renumber, and the chain green
the renumbered row uses a genuinely free id, and the vacated number is not reused anywhere
citations of the moved row's old id are found by grep and rewired, or their absence is stated
node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0 AND BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0
prohibitions: do not add a next-id allocator or any CLI verb — out of scope (D1)
do not add a second parse of docs/backlog.md; reuse the existing row walk
do not delete the existing counting assertion to make room — read it and keep it if it is still true
do NOT regenerate plugin trees, release manifest, runtime mirror or the ledger — the last cell owns all four (D9)

trace.outcome: Issue #49 fixed, but not the way it was reported. It asked for a lock on a max+1 allocator; no allocator exists, and the two duplicate rows were authored a day apart on different branches, each computing 'next free' from a snapshot predating the other — a lock would have prevented nothing. The missing piece was a uniqueness CHECK, now in the chain, reusing the existing row walk rather than adding a second parser. Run red first it named P50 on lines 23 and 31 of the committed file; the later row was renumbered to a genuinely free P70 with the vacated number not reused, and a citation sweep confirmed no live pointer needed rewiring. The existing assertion that counts duplicates honestly was kept after reading it — counting and refusing are different concerns — with a comment and two new tests recording why.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "The check, run BEFORE any repair, on the committed file: 'DUPLICATE docs/backlog.md id \"P50\" appears on 2 rows: line 23, line 31' / 'backlog_uniqueness --check: FAIL — 1 duplicated id(s)', exit 1. The duplicate was live in the repo, committed, and had been for five days.",
  "reported_cause_was_wrong": "The issue reported ids allocated 'max + 1' with concurrent readers colliding, and asked for locking. There is NO allocator code anywhere: backlog.mjs only parses, counts, ranks and badges the markdown table, and `bee backlog add` writes a different store entirely (the friction feed). The id rule lives in prose and is executed by an agent editing a table. The two P50 rows were authored A DAY APART on different branches — 2026-07-17 (3cc1e91) and 2026-07-18 (560b81e) — each computing 'next free' from a snapshot predating the other. A lock would have prevented nothing. What was missing was a CHECK, and that is what shipped.",
  "green_evidence": "backlog_uniqueness --check: every docs/backlog.md ID is unique, exit 0. Chain 82 suites green both with and without the agent-name prefix, re-run independently by the orchestrator.",
  "repair": "Renumbered the LATER row (2026-07-18, 'A Codex session runs bee with the same experience as Claude Code', feature codex-native-runtime-v2) from P50 to P70. P70 is genuinely free — the highest id in the file was P69, and P70 had never been used or vacated. The vacated number is not reused, per the backlog's own stated rule.",
  "citation_sweep": "grep over docs/, README.md and .bee/ for the old id found no LIVE pointer. The only hits are the kept earlier P50 row, the generated decision index and the append-only decisions log (both stating 'P50 added done', true when written — rewriting them would falsify history, not fix a pointer), and this feature's own CONTEXT.md and cell file describing the bug. Stated rather than assumed.",
  "no_second_parser": "The check reuses the existing row walk: rankBacklog's inline loop was extracted into a shared private walker that both it and the new findDuplicateBacklogIds call. rankBacklog and featureBacklogRank tests pass unchanged, so the extraction is behavior-identical.",
  "judgement_on_the_pinned_assertion": "The existing assertion that readBacklogCounts 'counts duplicate IDs honestly (row-by-row, dedup is grooming prose)' was KEPT, not rewritten — the worker read it and established that readBacklogCounts only tallies the Status column and holds no opinion about id uniqueness, so the assertion remains true. A comment now records why, and two new tests sit beside it making explicit that counting honestly and refusing in the chain are different concerns rather than contradictory ones.",
  "chain_wiring": "New scripts/backlog_uniqueness.mjs following the established --check idiom of ledger_parity.mjs, wired into run_verify's EXTRA_SUITES and pinned in test_verify_manifest's MANDATORY_SUITES and MANDATORY_SUITE_ARGS; SUITE_FLOOR_COUNT 64 -> 65, discovery now 82.",
  "orchestrator_review": "Both verify invocations and the uniqueness check re-run independently after the orchestrator performed the whole feature's derived-artifact regeneration. Confirmed exactly one P50 row and one P70 row remain. One flagged diagnostic dismissed: an unused assertRejects import in test_backlog_capture.mjs is pre-existing at HEAD, not introduced. The cell's own verify was red when the worker reported, entirely because both sibling cells edit vendored lib files and the four derived artifacts were deferred by design (D9) — the orchestrator ran that regeneration and both cells then verified green.",
  "deviation_accepted": "A new file, scripts/backlog_uniqueness.mjs, was not in the cell's declared files list but is required to expose the check to the chain; no existing script did. It follows the ledger_parity/okf_specs_fence idiom exactly."
}
